title: Using enclosing class name as function argument name leads to compile error
body:
I have a case where a function argument has the same name as a function argument:

```
file `test.sv`

class foo;

  function void some_function(int foo);
  endfunction

endclass
```

When I compile this with `verilator --binary test.sv`, I get:

```
%Error: test.sv:3:35: syntax error, unexpected TYPE-IDENTIFIER, expecting IDENTIFIER or randomize
    3 |   function void some_function(int foo);
      |                                   ^~~
%Error: Cannot continue
```

This works in a commercial tool.
